sources.citation: Python Software Foundation. The Python Tutorial. 2024.
MIT OCW 6.0001 Intro to CS in Python, Fall 2016.
VanderPlas. A Whirlwind Tour of Python. O'Reilly, 2016.

learning_objectives: L1: Identify Python built-in types: int, float, str, bool, list, tuple, dict, set.
L1: State the purpose of def, return, if/elif/else, for, while, import.
L1: Define function, parameter, argument, scope, and module.
L2: Explain mutable vs immutable types with examples.
L2: Describe how for loops iterate over list vs range.
L2: Compare list comprehensions with for loops for readability and performance.
L3: Write a Python function returning mean, median, and mode of a number list.
L3: Debug a Python function with off-by-one, NameError, or TypeError.

concepts.title: Built-in Data Types
concepts.explanation: int: arbitrary precision. float: 64-bit IEEE 754. str: immutable Unicode. bool: True/False. list: mutable ordered []. tuple: immutable ordered (). dict: mutable key-value {}. set: mutable unordered unique. None: null singleton.
concepts.worked_example: nums=[1,2,3] is mutable: nums[0]=99 works. point=(3,4) is immutable: point[0]=99 raises TypeError.

concepts.title: Conditions and Loops
concepts.explanation: if/elif/else, for item in iterable, range(start,stop,step) — stop exclusive. while, break, continue.
concepts.worked_example: FizzBuzz: for i in range(1,101): print('FizzBuzz' if i%15==0 else 'Fizz' if i%3==0 else 'Buzz' if i%5==0 else i)

concepts.title: Defining Functions
concepts.explanation: def name(params): block; return value. Default params, *args (tuple), **kwargs (dict). Scope LEGB: Local, Enclosing, Global, Built-in. Functions are first-class objects.
concepts.worked_example: def mean(nums): return sum(nums)/len(nums) if nums else 0  # guards ZeroDivisionError

concepts.title: Comprehensions
concepts.explanation: List: [expr for item in iterable if condition]. Dict: {k:v for k,v in iterable}. Faster and more readable than equivalent for loop.
